---
title: "Hackathon Day 2 Kickoff!"
title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-position: top
  data-background-image: images/sanctuary-buildings.jpg
  data-background-size: cover
  data-background-opacity: "0.1"
author: "Menna Zayed"
institute: Department for Education
format:
  revealjs:
   theme: [dark, dfe-quarto-slides.scss]
   width: 1060
   height: 900
   minScale: 0.2
   maxScale: 3.0
   logo: images/DfE_logo_landscape.svg
   self-contained: true
---

## Hackathon Day 2 Kickoff!

::::: columns
::: {.column width="60%"}
-   Welcome back to Day 2 of the Pre-Stats Awayday Hackathon!
-   We hope you had a productive Day 1 and are ready to dive back into your projects.
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
![](images/hackathon_fred.png)
:::
:::::

## Recap of day 1

-   Project planning ✅
-   Data access and tool setup ✅
-   Initial coding and development ✅

## Goals for today

::::: columns
::: {.column width="60%"}
-   Finalize your projects

    -   Finalise building and testing your prototype
    -   Make sure your code is on the GitHub repo
    -   Make sure you have a detailed README file in your repo

-   Prepare your presentations

-   Present your work at the end of the day
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
![](images/frederick-coding.png){figure-align="center"}
:::
:::::

## Agenda for Day 2

![](images/agenda_day2.png){figure-align="center"}

## Presentation Tips

::::: columns
::: {.column width="60%"}
-   Keep it concise: 5 to 10 minutes total, including time for questions.

    -   Aim for around 7–8 minutes of presenting, leaving 2–3 minutes for Q&A.

-   Focus on key insights: Highlight the problem, your approach, and results.

-   Use visuals: Screenshots, charts, and code snippets can help convey your message.

-   Practice: Rehearse your presentation to ensure smooth delivery.

-   Be ready for questions: Anticipate questions and prepare answers in advance.

-   Make sure to email your slides to Menna by end of day today so we can prepare for the Stats Awayday.
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
![](images/fred-present.png){figure-align="center"}
:::
:::::

## Presentation schdule

{{< include presentations_timings.qmd >}}

## Let's Get Started!

-   Set up a Teams meeting for your Team check-in
-   This will be followed by 'Hacking session #4' where you can continue development with drop-in sessions available.

## Presentations

{{< include presentations_timings.qmd >}}

## Voting

-   Best Overall Project : Most impactful, well-executed, and innovative solution.

-   Most Technically Impressive: Showcases advanced coding, data handling, or integration.

-   Best Use of Data: Clever or insightful use of datasets to drive outcomes.

-   Most Creative Solution: Unique or unexpected approach to solving the problem.

-   Best Presentation/Demo: Clear, engaging, and professional delivery of the project.


## Closing remarks

-   Thank you for your hard work and creativity over the past two days!
-   We hope you enjoyed the hackathon and learned something new.
-   We look forward to seeing your projects at the Stats Awayday on 11th September.
-   Please remember to email your slides to Menna by the end of today.
-   We value your feedback! Please take a moment to fill out our [feedback form](https://forms.office.com/e/3b0a1c4d9f).
